summary: Read and analyzed <machine-local-path> (1).md without editing the target file. Verified SHA-256 matched the expected value. Focused review on the 20-agent validation protocol, same-SHA requirement, full-memory-read requirement, reset rules, and operational viability.
decisions: Treat the document SHA as verified: 4B8C7447D4048781935EC7D8B8223112953941BEB4060AA0661AD6D298AE4DE7.
Conclude the validation protocol is safety-strong but not operationally viable as a manual/live-memory process unless it is snapshot-based and orchestrated.
Identify same document SHA as necessary but incomplete without a memory snapshot/manifest SHA, because live SecondBrain drift can make validators review different inputs.
Identify full-memory-read as useful for coverage proof but insufficient for semantic comprehension unless paired with required evidence sets and machine-readable evidence reporting.
Identify reset rules as logically consistent but liveness-hostile without prevalidation, early-stop orchestration, and a final locked validation stage.
next_steps: Use a frozen validation bundle containing document SHA plus SecondBrain snapshot/manifest SHA before launching final 20 agents.
Require machine-readable verdict fields for FULL_MEMORY_READ, DOCUMENT_SHA, MEMORY_SNAPSHOT_SHA, EVIDENCE_CHECKED, VERDICT, and P0/P1 blockers.
Run cheap prevalidation before the final same-SHA 20-agent notarization round.
